channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >be a normie >complain about rising costs of living >bitch about billionaires >*unmask the balance sheet* >12%+ frivolou… — 80 replies — 320.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>be a normie >complain about rising costs of living >bitch about billionaires >*unmask the balance sheet* >12%+ frivolou…

Open thread • Replies: 80 • Updated: 2026-05-22 01:27 UTC

OP

>be a normie
>complain about rising costs of living
>bitch about billionaires
>*unmask the balance sheet*
>12%+ frivolous fees/month
Why can't normies budget? is it just low IQ? Like this Plebbitor spends $110 a month on completely frivolous expenses at his stage (haircuts, spotify)

Also stories from normies being shit with money.

Early replies (first 3)

62254520
>take on debt to go to university
>make $2,200 a month
lol lmao

62254533
>>62254481
How is it fucking possible to waste $96 on a fucking haircut a month? And $13 for a fucking spotify? Aren't there free unlockers which take like 3 minutes to install?

62254543
>retarded normie can't help hemorrhaging money on dumb bullshit
A tale as old as time. He could get rid of his gym membership and with just a couple months of savings buy dumbbells and a pullup bar. He can buy a $20 electric razor and just shave his head a couple times a month. Music is free, if you pay for it you're a dumbass nigger.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62256523
>>62256474
point is, your dollar used to stretch far enough not even 25 years ago, that no one would bat an eye at those purchases. also you would make more and more money, but that ended. now people are noticing that they are being robbed and noticing the jewish-coded system. and you guys spergout when the goys think it's bullshit. that your callous response is "you can't have those things/you're shit with money/you can't shop/etc"

pretty soon it'll be. $15 to wash your clothes at the laundromat??? Go down to the lake and rinse your clothes in fecal water. You're getting more and more retarded.

62256544
>>62256523
You're overspending multiple times what you should for certain services/commodities and when someone tries to tell you how to budget like a non-retard you sperg out about the collapse of the system. Get fucked Mr. Silver Spoon, you spend like a spoiled kid you get mocked like one.

62256554
>>62255027
The writing in the OP does not look like a woman's. Also, vaginas spent a lot more on their hair than $48 per visit, or whatever.

62256572
>living in current day in america is like living in a third world country! Slavery never went away, it just evolved!
>ORDERS 12 DOORDASHES AND DAILY STARBUCKS

62256579
>>62256544
How do I budget for exponential never ending inflation on my stagnant wage, dumb fucking cocksucker?

62256585
>>62256544
bro, anon, i love you, but stop condoning this shit already

62256781
>>62256585
He’s right. The system is fucked but most of the world would kill for $2500 a month and it’s absolutely enough if you can cook chicken and potatoes and not spend money like a woman

62256785
LLM thread lol 50 1pbitd

62256791
>>62254551
>>62256396
>I live in DC
>MM
Nice larp ranjesh now go cover yourself in jam for me boy.

62256797
>>62256791
>for you ESL's confused by this nobody in the history of american history has ever abbreviated 20m (20 million)
with 20mm
It isn't a socket you ESL faggots, now please please if you're gonna LLM spam and larp goto r9k

62256815
>>62256797
You’re not nearly as smart as you think you are. Take a deep breath

62256858
>>62256815
Yea okay Bybon, son of Phola

62256865
>>62254481
I spend 5$/month for my phone. If I go over data it just slows down a bit but I don't get charged more. I'm mostly on wifi anyway. Who pays $110/mo for a phone bill?

62256883
>>62254481
Squat rack, bench and weights pays for itself in 6 months but maybe he doesn't have the space so okay whatever. $50 haircut every two weeks idk maybe he's black and needs a slick fade or the homies will jump his ass. Seems like a waste o money when you have $0 in the bank and a razor would pay for itself in two weeks

62257079
>>62256781
Most of the world aren't relevant since they aren't living in America

62257084
>>62254802
>Normies are called cattle by Jews and other non-normies for a reason.
Cause their parents don't buy them a block of flats with tenants once they turn 18?

62257246
>>62256883
>$50 haircut every two weeks idk maybe he's black and needs a slick fade or the homies will jump his ass.
imagine trying to justify being a nigger

62257421
>>62255083
>The median net worth of most people in america age 25-35 is probably closer to 3-5K USD
be realistic

62257449
>>62256865
He doesn’t own his phone, so he’s paying some company like Verizon or T-Mobile $110 a month instead of buying it outright.
Normies are fucking retarded.

62257993
>>62256523
>$15 to wash your clothes at the laundromat???
lol the one I got to is $16 total actually, $8 to wash and $8 to dry but it is the only one within walking distance and i dont have a car. it's all white people here so they can get away with those kinds of prices

62258026
>>62254481
>$110 for a phone. Does this faggot pay for triple platinum 6G with 10Gbit up/down and free calls to Nauru and Tuvalu?
What the fuck?

62258176
>>62254481
>rent $425
Is this from 2010?

62258317
>>62255552
Bro if you weren't so midwit yourself you'd read that my post grants their IQ went down. Because it literally couldn't have done anything but gone down. I'm not arguing it didn't go down.

62258334
Thinly veiled reddit ad

Kill yourself

62258471
>>62254481
>Phone - 110
Faggot shit. I pay $39/month on T-mobile 12GB Max Data Prepaid (just turn it to WiFi mode whenever home)
>Bus - 30
Get a bike
>Gym - 75
Buy some free weights, bench, and rack from Craiglist
>Haircut x2 - 96
$50 haircuts twice a month? WTF?
>Spotify - 13
Learn to torrent
>Monthly Income - 2,200
This nigger is only making $26.4k a month? Even just the most basic low level security / receptionist job pays $40k per year!

What a faggot. OP image taker should KYS.

62258608
>>62256781
pic related. nobody wants to hear a peep from anarcho-capitalist retards and rabbis on this topic. you can't keep spinning the prices going up like this.

>>62257993
yeah i figured. i thought my example was hyperbolic. and you're just confirming it's already gotten worse than that. it's completely ridiculous, my example was already obsoleted.
> it's all white people here so they can get away with those kinds of prices
no, they really can't. that shit is dumb.

62260577
>>62256797
>I'm poor
>my blockfolio never showed MM

62260603
>>62254481
>monthly income: 2200
>next check: 1250
math aint mathing

62260614
>>62258608
Boomers built and put in place the system that gave them 25 cent cheeseburger meals. Zoomers were raised on the internet and subsequently expect the world to be handed to them without doing any work.
tl;dr zoomers are lazy crybabies.

62260618
>>62254543
>have 36,293 in debt
>cancel gym membership, sportify, stop getting haircuts and start shaving your head
literally everyone can tell that you cut your own hair, by the way
there is never a situation where it doesnt look like shit
anyway
>save 184/month
With this incredible advice, he'll be able to pay off his debt in a little over 16 years, assuming it's not accumulating interest (it is).

You're on the same level of retardation as boomers declaring that young people can't afford homes because they're spending all their money on avocado toast. Going to the gym and getting professional haircuts to not look like a sperg who cuts his own hair will unironically be a far better financial investment than saving a couple hundred dollars a month. Being fit and well groomed will go a long way toward getting a better job which is the only realistic advice you should be giving someone like this, not fucking
>well if you just cut your own hair for the next 20 years then you might save enough to pay down your debt

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 65 replies — 260.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-05-22 01:23 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62260221
>>62260218
where is that?

62260231
>>62260065
It makes it exponentially easier to get your foot in the door, but you can become Chad for a woman once she gets to know you even if you aren't really tall with perfect genetics, since they're susceptible to other markers like dominance, confidence, wit etc. The problem is in modern dating its very difficult to pass that first barrier since the genetic Chads can easily display their stuff to thousands of women at a time via apps and social media, effectively cutting off regular guys who can still do other stuff but can't pass that first looks check.

62260246
>>62260192
You sound like my brother, albeit he's married but his wife works too, and I know he makes 150k+ working a government job. But he takes vacations and spends everything so almost never invests.

I make like 100-120k but live off only 50-60k, and could probably go lower by 20k if I really pushed it before it started to get uncomfortable.

Its just one of those things, some people allow themselves to get used to certain luxuries and don't even consider taking them off the table, and everytime they make a bit more money they allow another bunch of expenses to creep up and so they never get anywhere. Not saying you should live frugal and never enjoy life either, but you gotta be able to big picture it to some degree. That said considering my brothers job is government he's probably set for life and doesn't really have to care, so I don't really blame him.

62260263
>>62260221
Big city.
Go look up the Pallasaides fire. When the fire just started “million dollar homes” were on fire. I saw the news and images and was like wow, I thought that was the ghetto. Turns out it was. Big cities are like this.

62260266
>>62260039
poor quality, filter bubble themed comic originating on 4chan? the author is an incel neet

62260267
>>62260183
>average pay is probably 20k-35k
nice larp retard

62260276
>>62260267
In small cities, yeah.
Average american salary is 65k. Without the top earners dragging it up, it is like 35k.
$60k in a small city is like $120k in a big city.

62260280
>>62260267
In your flyover shithole, why would there be bunch of $100k and up jobs pulling the average up

62260281
>>62260263
thanks, I was curious where you're from

62260287
>>62260231
>The problem is in modern dating its very difficult to pass that first barrier
It took me a while to finally pass that barrier and then i got rejected for my personality. I sort of accepted that I would end up living alone after that. The only women i get along with are autistic women and all of them are either taken or gay.

62260306
>>62260287
Sorry to hear that anon, we'll all end up alone anyways. But fucking whores isn't that bad either, I went through a phase in my mid 30's after having never partaken and its really not that different from fucking girls you naturally picked up, just with some upsides and downsides. I would suggest you at least do that a bit, be warned a lot of encounters will be dry but they're still real people and sometimes you can click with a whore and its a lotta fun (just don't fall for them).

62260355
>>62259935
25k is way too much of a budget for a single man. You can do it on 15k easily, but I budgeted for 20k/yr at 3.33% withdrawal to have some extra cushion. The trick is to take however long the FIRE projection is and add 1 year, use everything gained extra in that 1 year to get a modest house, and then you have basically no expenses since you don't have to go to a job and can cook/garden. Hopefully divorce laws change sometime before I die so I can marry and have a kid and not lose my chill lifestyle.

62260356
Yes

62260375
>>62259935
This is essentially what everyone dreams of "making it" being financially secured without sacrificing your time and labor and enjoy having leisure. Guys that are single have a huge leap forward towards attaining this freedom, and once you obtain it why the flying fuck would you throw it away for a warm hole and bear extra responsibilities that'll inevitably get you back in a cage?

62260376
>>62259997

62260411
Dude I’m completely impressed. Make it in just ten years after leaving the college meme.

Meanwhile some other going to college in the 40s to upgrade their skill

62260418
>>62260355
> 25k is way too much of a budget for a single man.
Gotta live in a nice place, man
>You can do it on 15k easily
Pathetic. lmao. Lower class life.

62260424
Imagine living in this realm never experiencing true love and happiness. I hope OP die child less and surrounded by funko pops.

62260426
>>62259935
I wont be relaxing. Ill just not have a boss and only take on projects I want to do. I wont even need to make a profit.

62260431
>>62259980
Thats not even true. You would only split assets you aquire while married. If you have a coreectly structured trust you can marry

62260470
everything is fine except the cook for parents part

62260482
>>62260470
Why? Do you dislike your parents?

Maybe he just enjoys cooking and is good at it.

62260500
>>62259938
>>62259945
This is the jewbait. Dont have kids whitey, please. Its soooo bad. Meanwhile childless men want to kms after 3 years if being jobless and 'living the dream'

62260502
>>62259968
Also jewbait and not true. Look up premarital assets retard.

62260509
>>62260418
>Pathetic. lmao. Lower class life.
Immigrant striver post. A discerning male has inherent nobility, he can forego material affectation. Only bug-souls don't understand this.

62260516
>>62260025
You dont pay child support if 50/50 custody and wife didnt already depend on your income for years upon years/decades. You are either jewish or brainwashed by jews.

62260542
Hello, I’m Seeker.

Today, my parents left Shanghai and returned to their rural hometown. After sending them off at the train station, my wife and I took the subway home in silence. The apartment we once felt proud of now just feels like a very expensive cage.

Before leaving, my mom filled the freezer with meat she had already cut into portions. My dad bought bags of bread and stacked them in the fridge.

At first glance, it looked caring.

But honestly, it also felt tragic.

Because two elderly farmers from a rural village are now preparing emergency food supplies for their unemployed son who bought into the dream everyone told us would lead to a better life.

For years, society kept repeating the same formula:
Study hard.
Move to the big city.
Buy property.
Work harder than everyone else.
Endure now, enjoy later.

We believed it.

So my wife and I poured our entire savings into a $1 million apartment in Shanghai. People said housing prices would only rise. People said owning property meant security. People said sacrificing your youth was normal because one day it would all pay off.

Instead, shortly after buying the apartment, we both lost our jobs.

Now every month feels like paying rent to a bank for a future that may never come.

The cruelest part is realizing how many people were probably fooled the same way.

You spend your healthiest years chasing stability.
Then one economic downturn wipes everything out.
Suddenly your education, your overtime, your exhaustion, your sacrifices — none of it protects you.

My parents noticed immediately.

Before this, we used to give them money every month because they helped raise our child while we worked. But once my mother understood our financial situation, she refused to accept anything anymore.

She lied and said groceries were cheap.

But poor people always know exactly how much things cost.

62260546
Chinese parents rarely say “I love you.”

Instead, they quietly lower their own living standards so their children can survive.

This morning, we brought our 2-and-a-half-year-old to kindergarten before taking my parents to the station. Watching them struggle with subway signs and smartphone payments made something painfully clear to me:

The older generation sacrificed their bodies to escape poverty.

Our generation sacrificed our lives chasing prosperity.
And somehow both generations still ended up exhausted.

Before leaving, I taught my parents how to use mobile payments because modern China barely functions without smartphones anymore. My father kept forgetting the steps. My mother pretended to understand so she wouldn’t burden me further.

That moment broke me inside.

Tonight, I’ll cook the chicken wings my mom prepared before leaving.

The freezer is full.

But the illusion that hard work guarantees security is completely gone.

62260570
>>62259935
Just wait for your dad to nag you for some little kids as a bonus and for the legacy and neighbours boosting about the beautiful made it family with kids, then your chaos can begin again, it never end, the suffer keep coming.

62260605
FIRE graduate here.
$2.5M worth, living off the $35k-$40k/year it brings in.
Single and happy to be.
Still go to 'work' at my RE investing job 3x/week so you could say I'm 'working rich'.

Attained independence @41 y.o. Currently 55.
Own my own little condo, rent out 10 more.

item.title: NTDOY — 41 replies — 164.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NTDOY

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-05-22 01:23 UTC

OP

Currently Nintendo stock is extremely mispriced. All the short-term doom and gloom is priced in, ignoring the company's tremendous long-term upside. While it's not some 10x your money in a week meme coin, at this point it's a sleeper stock to hold you should know about.

Early replies (first 3)

62259388
>>62259371
its not mispriced. they make shitty games for 80+$. no one wants to buy their crap because its shit.

62259405
>NTDOY baggie
KWAB

62259407
>>62259371
not misspriced
no point in shilling your bags here btw
>>62259388
this and console era is coming to an end. Future will be only game stations with steam like platforms. retards still go for the ps5 by some sort of pavlovian reflex. it's just a standardized computer with a standardized controller. Nintendo shined for a while 30 years ago now it has become so cringe i can't stand their adds like wtf is even this shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62259497
Nintendo stock deserves to be in the shitter because only like 5% of their revenue is licensing. They should have done everything in their power to acquire the pokemon IP outright.

62259526
>>62259371
I actually bought 500 shares this morning. Its definitely a pretty easy win at these prices, most of the anons on this board are brown poors unfortunately. Their entire brokerage accounts are worth like 900 dollars

62259529
>>62259371
>NTDOY
>Mispriced
Lmao. Nintendo makes games for exclusively for millennials that suck cock and eat shit. Zoomers play Roblox and Fortnite and the only thing they have going for them are movies now.

62259551
>>62259497
Very fair criticism, but counter-argument, Nintendo has licensed their IPs in the past with catastrophic results, and learned keeping things in-house allows Nintendo to make sure everything meets its very high quality control standards.

62259554
i'm gonna buy a fpga setup and there is nothing OP and his bags can do about it. If nintendo weren't retarded they would make some dedicated console with all the SNES / N64 hell even gamecube hits easily affordable they would make an absolute killing. Only now FPGA exists and after trying for 20 years to emulate old games without lag and shit it's too late

62259566
>>62259371
Yep, bought the dip as well. I easily see it going to 2x my investment in less than a year’s time.

62259574
>>62259554
>If nintendo weren't retarded they would make some dedicated console with all the SNES / N64 hell even gamecube hits easily affordable they would make an absolute killing.
Oops!

62259588
>>62259574
quality is very inconsistent, input lag, limited catalog

62259594
>>62259588

62259599
>>62259429
You are embarrassing. Take the L, shut Up and leave the discussion. Talk about stuff you know at least an ounce about.

62259722
>>62259526
>>62259566

62259729
>>62259371
My dad works here

62259742
>>62259729
Tell him the shareholders want him to work harder

62259746
I hope it works out for you bro but I don't want to fund what I see as a dying company in a dying market.

62259808
>>62259746
Mid 1980s: people predict video game market dead due to major crash and low-quality games
Late 1980s: people predict video game market dead due to arcade cooldown and home console "fad"
Mid 1990s: people predict video game market dead due to "CD Rom bubble panic" as commercial failures like Atari Jaguar and 3DO make people think industry is imploding
Early 2000s: people predict video game market dead due to 3D games becoming the norm and production budgets exploding
Mid 2000s: people predict video game market dead due to popularity of browser and web-based alternatives
Early 2010s: people predict video game market dead due to widespread popularity of mobile and social media gaming alternatives
Mid/Late 2010s: people predict video game market dead due to massive DLC, microtransactions, and "loot box" gambling dynamics
Early 2020s: people predict video game market dead due to "battle royale bubble" with high concentration in games like Fortnite
2026: random faggot on /biz/ predicts video game market dead because "this time it's different"

62259814
>>62259371
Switch 2 owner here. The new console sold very well due to the positive sentiment created by the Switch 1, and the hardware is certainly most pleasant to hold and see. Other than that, it’s a total disappointment.

>no games, the current lineup is both paltry and boring as fuck
>battery is kill after 2 hours
>storage on the console is rather low
>almost every 3rd party game is a fucking "game key card" that combines the worst of physical (having to carry a cartridge) and the worst of digital (takes the little storage you have)
>hardware self-destructs even if you take great care of it, my left joycon stick looks like it took 15 years of beatings after just a few months (it never happened with anything else I own)

Because of these problems, I’m buying 10 times fewer games on the Switch 2 compared to the Switch 1, and I will not buy a Switch 3 in the future given the more than mediocre treatment we get here.

TL;DR Nintendo gives the illusion of doing well with their numbers, but they’re actually shitting the bed. Disappointing performance will ensue if you are a long term shareholder.

62259828
>>62259814
The console is still in its infancy. Every criticism you have is fixable. New console-seller titles are inevitable. Imagine the Switch 2 Pro with battery and storage upgrades, etc.

62259884
>>62259808
Video games aren't dead. Consoles and reliance on proprietary IPs is. Gaming continues in phones for casuals and PC indies for the hardcore crowd. It's funny because had you invested in phones and social media instead of gaming companies since the early 2010s you'd be massively better off than an investment in Nintendo. Who's talking about "it'll be different this time"?

And anyway, yes, the world is different in 2026 than it was in 1980. Investment is about the future, not staring at previous success and insisting it'll happen again. What is Nintendo doing that could possibly replicate their boom in the 1980s?

62259900
>>62259828
It’s fixable, but will it be fixed? So far, nothing indicates that Nintendo takes the problem seriously, especially with the "no games" problem. Looks like Nintendo will just release four or five decent AAA games through the entire console’s lifespan (one Mario, one Zelda or whatever, and these are not even announced) and then call it a day.

62259983
>>62259371
Why would people be hungry for buying an ever increasing in price console (due to RAM) with zero new Zelda or Mario on it?

62260004
>>62259814
Dude, SW2 is a stop gap filler console until the real one (with a new hardware gimmick) wil be ready, the big games (mainline mario/zelda) will come out very late to be also used as launch titles for the next.
This is wii u/gba/3ds all over again.

62260032
>>62259371
>making this thread again
Please god nuke the third world

62260035
>>62259371
I agree. Not because of vidya, but because of Pokémon cards. The market cap of all PSA10 graded Pokémon cards is over $75B. Nintendo's market cap os only $53B. It makes no sense that the company that makes the cards is worth so much less than the cards themselves.

62260053
>>62260004
Nintendo could actually get away with it (releasing a Switch 3, 4, 5, etc. forever) by not being so retarded and underwhelming… Instead they will be forced to invent something else that may or may not be successful.

62260188
>>62259371
Thanks

62260304
>>62259371
agreed p/e is slurpable, nintendo aint going away

62260335
>>62259884
already priced in

62260390
Apple is apple

62260407
I bought a Rog ALLY X and it’s 100000x better than the switch I had for my son. You know the target audience? My kid uses the Ally to watch YouTube play actual fun games. He cares more about Spider-Man than bing bing wahoo slop. Nintendo failed to capture the audience by marketing to childless manchildren just like Disney Adults. They have no market share and sell an inferior product. Pokémon still looks and plays like an indie game from 2005. (lol)

Enjoy your bags tendie you get what you deserve.

62260606
>>62260407

item.title: $14,930,352 usdt in a chrome browser extension — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
$14,930,352 usdt in a chrome browser extension

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-22 01:30 UTC

OP

will probably buy an RV in europe and go travelling around the continent
my main goal is to eat tasty italian and french food
maybe buy a reasonable house ($2-3million GBP) 20 min from London

who else here has #madeit?

Early replies (first 3)

62260569
>>62260563
did you pay your taxes?

62260578
>not holding your usdt before it breaks out of multi-year $1.00 resistance into price discovery
ngmi

62260595
>>62260563
umm ackshaully it's "in" the encrypted private key stored in a database file in your browser's local storage

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62260608
I fumbled $9MM.

62260612
>>62260563
I can haz 5k?

62260626
put it in a hardware wallet, retard

item.title: TNN wasn’t a meme — 8 replies — 13.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
TNN wasn’t a meme

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-22 00:56 UTC

OP

Link cucks are butthurt that all their meme “partnerships” turned out to be giant nothingburgers that benefit only ChainlinkLabs while having ZERO effect on the price of the token

Early replies (first 3)

62256900
>>62256898
What's wrong with that? I bought the coin to support Sergei and the team. It's not a lottery ticket

62256904
>>62256900
Based and cuckpilled

Sergey deserves a new penthouse in NYC

62256916
>>62256898
My theory is that originally they were going to funnel the value through the token but the community being made up of super autistic Nazi’s made them change course to fuck over the community as hard as possible

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62256935
>>62256916
lol I might actually sell now. This is the best FUD I’ve heard in 8 years

62257479
>>62256935
Me too sister! That was like super duper good FUD you know what I mean? It makes me wanna market sell my whole stack like right now! Le hecking nazis!

62257587
MESSAGE TO ALL LINK MARINES

Please don't listen to the FUD, the project is extremely solid and will pump beyond your imagination in 2027. Keep holding my fellow warriors!

62260457
>>62256916
this
the "link marines" are odious, contemptible creatures
it was an easy sell to get sergey to switch his company's goal to be simply fucking them over
and the best part is, it's 100% legal
because you are just TOKEN HOLDERS. not shareholders, with legal protections
sucks to suck, stinkies.

62260543
>>62256916
Just realized I had this same line of thought internally but never paid much mind to it. It was around the time of the huge HR foid salaries that kept getting spammed here

item.title: If you were sub 120 IQ and had to start over career wise RIGHT NOW, what career field would you get into with the goal o… — 7 replies — 9.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you were sub 120 IQ and had to start over career wise RIGHT NOW, what career field would you get into with the goal o…

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-22 00:48 UTC

OP

If you were sub 120 IQ and had to start over career wise RIGHT NOW, what career field would you get into with the goal of maximizing against AI replacement, that ISN'T a trade?

Early replies (first 3)

62260419
>>62260402
>that ISN'T a trade?
kek'd

62260433
>>62260402
Health care seems pretty safe in my opinion. People still need to be seen in person by nurses or get scanned/tested by techs for a myriad of endless health issues in our increasingly unhealthy population.
One huge problem though is nearly every single medical facility in America, even health core courses, mandate the covid vax because we're all mindraped goykike'd cattle slaves.

62260473
>>62260433
Health Care is a trade in reality.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62260481
>>62260433
It seems like a good option, I don't know if I could handle being around "customers" all day, but thought about doing xray tech

62260486
>>62260402
Seriously become a tradie and build your local data center.

62260523
>>62260402
Production, shipping, transportation, storage, delivery… of physical resources that are vital to the AI economy (water, energy, rare earth elements, silicon, gallium, germanium, helium, palladium, tantalum, copper, bromine, lithium, etc.).

AI needs all that, in gigantic quantities.

If you are in America, this is considered a major strategic issue for the government and the largest companies out there, so you could even get great funding if you can provide these resources to the major AI players in America.

Don’t fight AI, fight for AI.

62260526
>>62260486
How?

item.title: >“When we came out of COVID, suddenly the big bosses in the corporate casino culture changed the game,” Basso said. — 3 replies — 4.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>“When we came out of COVID, suddenly the big bosses in the corporate casino culture changed the game,” Basso said.

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-22 00:54 UTC

OP

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sIOhaX61JYk

>“When we came out of COVID, suddenly the big bosses in the corporate casino culture changed the game,” Basso said. “They needed to make up some money that they had lost and it was, by playing catch-up ball, in my opinion, they decided to go for the whales rather than the regular folks.”

>“I did not like overcharging people and every month you had to look at it and say, gosh, we’ve got to raise prices,” Basso said. “You do it a couple of times, it bothers you. When you’re faced with doing that repetitively, I want nothing to do with it.”
The COVID stimulus checks devalued the dollar is what happened, Doctor. If the ingredients you buy to make the food is higher, and the profit margin % is still the same, what you are essentially doing is just selling it at the same fair market value but just under a different price tag. That is not "overcharging". It's surprising that someone of your age and business expertise doesn't understand that.

Early replies (first 3)

62260108
Someone post that cuck faggot Charles gorging his fat face there

62260530
>>62260037
The real catch with this place isn't the portion size, it's how fucking bad the food is. Yes, I got spanked. No, the food was not good.

62260537
>>62260530
>I got spanked.
I'm surprised nobody sued for sexual harassment or anything.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Samsung chip workers will get an average $340,000 bonus as AI profits soar — 3 replies — 2.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Samsung chip workers will get an average $340,000 bonus as AI profits soar

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-22 00:17 UTC

OP

Samsung Electronics will distribute about 40 trillion won ($26.6 billion) in bonuses to chip division employees this year after striking a tentative agreement with its labor union, according to Bloomberg. Using the proposed terms and analyst projections for 2026 operating profit, Bloomberg calculated the average payout at 513 million won, the equivalent of about $340,000. The total average compensation across Samsung was 158 million won in 2025, per a company filing.

WARNING /BIZ/!!!!!!!!!! IGNORE THE OP TEXT.
N A N O ( X N O ) IS GOING TO MOON SOON. IT WILL FLIP BITCOIN

THEY DON'T WANT YOU IN THEY ONLY WANT REDDIT IN

THEY WILL DELETE ALL THREADS THAT EVEN MENTION N A N O !!!!!!!!!!!!!

CHECK ARCHIVES CHECK ARCHIVES CHECK ARCHIVES!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

The agreement, subject to a union ratification vote running May 22 through May 27, calls for Samsung to direct 10.5% of operating profit into stock bonuses along with a separate 1.5% cash component, according to Bloomberg. The program runs for 10 years, contingent on the company meeting profit thresholds. One-third of the stock award can be liquidated right away, with the rest parceled out in installments across the next two years, Bloomberg reported. The first payout is expected in early 2027.

Early replies (first 3)

62259625
>>62259603
Holy FUCK YOU OCKIVNC NCD ME BOI
IM ALL IN NAO

IM A NANOBOT NAO

62259883
>>62259603
Qrd on this coin humble saars

62260453
Sk hynix chads got more like 800k lmao

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: HODL and buy more BTC. — 57 replies — 16.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
HODL and buy more BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-05-21 22:02 UTC

OP

If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?

>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway. I’d rather have BTC long term than let my money rot in a savings account.

Early replies (first 3)

62245757
i get 5btc later for cheaper but thanks for the advice!

62245760
>>62245741
Too eqrly but yeah and avoid cex or at least take it out as fast as u can!

62246438
>>62245757
How much BTC are you holding right now anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62253652
>>62253489

This is the way. And diversify. Coins like Monero and Nerva are private like cash, and in the case of Nerva it can't even be centralized/pooled due to how it changes algorithms. TRX is another for the list because it has the highest concentration of stablecoin transactions. Don't stick to just one asset: treat them like stocks.

62254516
>>62250313
What's your basis for that?

62254625
>>62246438
This much, and heres my home address. Theres a wrench in the top drawer in the kitchen, my room is upstairs and to the left.

62256658
>>62253489
I don't think we can stake btc
>>62253652
Why people are crazy about monero?

62256668
>>62254625
You're poor and you don't have even 1 btc in your wallet.

62256679
>>62252472
You should buy btc and treat as your savings

62256924
>>62253652
Monero is fine but nerva? The nerve

62256993
>>62256658
You probably don’t know much about Babylon yet, that’s why you’re asking that.
And if they’re into Monero then yeah, privacy is obviously the priority.

62256998
>>62250366
Btc is the king!!

62257164
>>62252912
What the hell are you talking about?

62257170
>>62256993
If it's centralised services, it's a big NO

62257173
>>62247257
Add ICP and Ethereum

62257204
>>62257170
Nah. Don’t trust centralized offerings anymore.
We’ve already seen how those end up. Fully decentralized + self custody + native BTC is the way kek.

62257209
>>62252439
A lot of people will realize that too late desu.

62257223
>>62245741
fact, he's still hodling

62257253
>>62257223
Is he satoshit?

62257345
>>62257173
ICp? Lol

62257392
>>62257204
but everything in defi is hype

62257440
>>62257223

62257675
>>62245741
>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway.
BTC doesn't improve any of this. Its cheaper and easier to spend gold.

62257679
>>62246878
It was decided that BTC would never scale. Self custody is impossible on BTC. Go ahead and try it. I swear when I make it I am going to hunt small blockers with a rifle.

62257680
it's boomer coin

62258573
>>62257679
will your cut your d?

62258577
>>62257680

62258754
>>62257223

62258775
>>62257679
nonsense. It's all in your head

62258793

62259397

lets hold hands and buy in Oct

62259745
>>62259397

62260090
>>62259745

Early replies (first 3)

its a dysgenic thing, who knows

62244608

62244615
>>62244587

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62248532
>>62246964

>>62246257
Just mention XRP in a Link thread and he will appear.
Like I said, he is most likely just another larp by the same schizophrenic nutcase who spams under the Thomas/Adem larp.

62248535
We do this because you linkfags keep shitting on XRP forcing that shitty "boom" meme

Go fuck yourselves

62248561
>>62247642
Pic is fud spam from the spring of 2020, when Link had been beating the market for nearly two years straight.
Price has nothing to do with the fud spam.

If you read the fud, it's about Coinbase teaming up with Compound to make their own oracle, making Chainlink not needed.
No points for guessing what happened soon after.

62248593
>>62248561
such KABOOOOOOOOOM
fxckings bullish link

62248620
>>62248535

Also the boom thing is from xerpies spamming that shit in Swift tweet comments.

62250765
>>62246964
fud harder cuck, massa's gonna liquidate you
>>62247391
use a filter bitch

62250818
>>62248561

you lost 50% since then while every other market hit all time highs

62251273

62251282

item.title: >US government to invest $2 billion in quantum computing companies >Quantum stocks soars >He still haven't bought in NGM… — 12 replies — 7.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>US government to invest $2 billion in quantum computing companies >Quantum stocks soars >He still haven't bought in NGM…

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-21 23:53 UTC

OP

>US government to invest $2 billion in quantum computing companies
>Quantum stocks soars
>He still haven't bought in
NGMI!

Early replies (first 3)

62258925
>>62258361
>buy AFTER it soars
Never change, /piz/

62258931
>>62258361
State run capitalism is based and communist pilled. Buying more Dell stock!

62258940
While I'm by no means an insider, I have had the pleasure of talking with various people in the quantum field recently. It's still a good time to buy in, but some kind of ETF or tracker, not specific companies - because it's simply too unclear who will win out. But the industry as a whole will be going places. Probably not as far as the AI stuff because it will have use cases that are too specific, but it is coming.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62258962
>the only stocks that perform are the ones that get government gibs
so this.......... is the power............................................................... of the free market........................................ whoa.............................................

62259014
>>62258361
>$2 billion
Holy Moly !! A couple of B-nuts just hit the Quantum market, the surge might even be detectable, if you do x10 leverage right now you can get an easy 5% ROI from this trade!
Thanks for posting this OP!!

62259775
>>62259014
First off, this was enough to get my quantum stocks out of the red and give me a profit of 22% in a single day. It was a decent pump. Secondly, $2B is nothing compared to what will come. Stop being a faggot and join in

62259789
>>62258940
>because it's simply too unclear who will win out.
I've been feeling like this is probably true. Every quantum company right now seems to be trying a completely different apporach from each other. Unfortunately I already injected too much money in individual companies and can't get into ETFs atm.

62259797
>>62258361
It's a bubble.

62259803
>>62259797
Doesn't matter even if it is one. This time I'm early and I can make a shit ton of money.

62260094
Reminder that quantum has no path to profitability ever.

62260383
>>62260094
Elaborate

62260391
>>62258962
Been that way for a while, just more obvious routes of handing out the gibs now. Amazon, for example, got a sweetheart deal with the post office in 2012 that meant they effectively paid ~$1 per package no matter the size or weight, anywhere from 1/10 to 1/40th of normal shipping costs.

Anyways, to make a long story short, you need to read up on Keeta (KTA). They have something coming out tomorrow. You have limited time to read about it before tomorrow. My discord channel just gave the green light
>You have limited time to read about my shitcoin
Please sar very quickly don't think just buy quickly
>This will likely be my only post ever about this

62260334
>shilling keeta
>in the big 26

I'm not sure why you would now shill it again unless you're a bagholder.

62260434
Last message. The hate on here is why I have not been on here for so long. You all seem so poor in attitude and dollars. Good luck to those out there who are not ignorant.

item.title: I am so fucking bored of my wfh job. I've been in it for a few years and in recent months my responsibilities have dried… — 5 replies — 5.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I am so fucking bored of my wfh job. I've been in it for a few years and in recent months my responsibilities have dried…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-22 00:32 UTC

OP

I am so fucking bored of my wfh job. I've been in it for a few years and in recent months my responsibilities have dried up.

The job hasn't contributed to my CV for a few years and everything moves so slowly. I thought I'd be in the job temporarily at first but then I found out when I started that I could permanently work from home. Now I'm in a golden handcuffs type situation where I save over £1k per month but I am a bottom of the barrel six figure purgatory peon.

I could move sideways into another job that is similar but maybe has more responsibility. But I'd have to move to London and live in a tiny, shitty flat and spend so much on rent and go in the office a few times a week.

But the spark for anything related to a career is gone. I'm not a normie. I'm an ugly beta so I will never ever be promoted.

My life is a social void (and always has been) and I don't do anything productive outside of work. Everything feels like an unimaginably long slog away.

I experiment with AI coding but making slop isn't rewarding and when everything can make things, the difficulty simply shifts to being an online marketer.

Taking part in the creator economy is a non-starter for someone as ugly as me.

I tried to get into video games again but I'm seriously done with them and have been for years. Reading books feels pointless.

People make fun of Asians on Twitter for "striving" but it's simply the rational path in life to optimise your career around a few set of skills and a relatively narrow band of industries and institutions (none of which I accessed).

I'm in my 30s. Physical decay hasn't come yet but it's on the horizon. All the women my age are busted and I have no options because no woman has ever been attracted to me anyway.

Early replies (first 3)

62260064
>me 5 years from now

62260088
>>62260014
you make this thread every week. anyways im 36 and have been wfh since march of 2020. never going back to DA OFFICE if i can help it.

62260292
>>62260014
>paid to do nothing
>whines
Fuck you

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62260297
haha yeah it sucks to sit in your underwear clicking buttons andm akeing 200k a year haha you should quit and move to london or whatever.

62260488
Yeah same boat man, making too much money to leave but literally doing nothing all day

cuz if so, eat shit

62258569
>>62257367
One day you'll wake up and the realization of all that wasted time and cost opportunity spent on post ironic memes will hit you all at once and you'll blow your brains out. Along with every other bagholder who killed themselves as well

62258599
>>62257367
I have 60k Link staked in the community pool and 23k not staked. I'll be staking those when the pool expands. Also I have 629K SXT staked and 139K FHE staked, that I got for nothing from Build rewards.
I partly (though by no means fully) have fudders to thank for my diamond hands. It was abundantly clear from their obsession that Link was the one. And here we are. Nearly there.

62259006
>>62258599
83k LINK is a fat stack. Why did you not sell at $50? Could have bought literally any blue chip stock and have $10M+ by now. Anyway, I'm jealous. I have worked up to just over 60k now, though I had way less back then or I definitely would have sold.
